text.title: Banking Core — Data Residency Rules Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed when banking-core-data-residency-rules is applied incorrectly. Each entry has a symptom (`description`), a root cause (`reason`), and a corrective fix (in `note`).

antipattern.description: Cloud auto-replication ships PII to a US region 'for resilience'.
antipattern.reason: Default cloud config; nobody mapped the residency envelope before enabling.
antipattern.note: Fix: Pin all regions in IaC; block global replication; require explicit per-jurisdiction opt-in.

antipattern.description: New SaaS vendor onboarded; processes EU customer data from a US back-office.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add residency-impact review as a procurement gate; reject vendors that cannot meet residency.

antipattern.description: Schrems II TIA never reviewed after 2025 EDPB clarifications.
antipattern.reason: TIA seen as one-time, not annual.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add annual TIA review to compliance calendar; on regulatory update, force re-review.
